segments.text: Then Jesus told his disciples a parable to show them that they should always pray and not give up.
He said:
In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor cared what people thought.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And there was a widow in that town who kept coming to him with the plea,
Grant me justice against my adversary.
segments.speaker: Widow

segments.text: For some time he refused. But finally he said to himself,
Even though I don't fear God or care what people think,
segments.speaker: Unjust Judge

segments.text: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will see that she gets justice, so that she won't eventually come and attack me!
segments.speaker: Unjust Judge

segments.text: And the Lord said,
Listen to what the unjust judge says.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And will not God bring about justice for his chosen ones, who cry out to him day and night? Will he keep putting them off?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, he will see that they get justice, and quickly. However, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Pharisee and the Tax Collector

segments.text: To some who were confident of their own righteousness and looked down on everyone else, Jesus told this parable:
Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed:
God, I thank you that I am not like other people—robbers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
segments.speaker: Pharisee

segments.text: I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.
segments.speaker: Pharisee

segments.text: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said,
God, have mercy on me, a sinner.
segments.speaker: Tax Collector

segments.text: I tell you that this man, rather than the other, went home justified before God. For all those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: People were also bringing babies to Jesus for him to place his hands on them. When the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.
But Jesus called the children to him and said,
Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Rich and the Kingdom of God

segments.text: A certain ruler asked him,
Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: Ruler

segments.text: Why do you call me good?
No one is good—except God alone.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You know the commandments: 'You shall not commit adultery, you shall not murder, you shall not steal, you shall not give false testimony, honor your father and mother.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All these I have kept since I was a boy,
he said.
segments.speaker: Ruler

segments.text: When Jesus heard this, he said to him,
You still lack one thing. Sell everything you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he heard this, he became very sad, because he was very wealthy.
Jesus looked at him and said,
How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who heard this asked,
Who then can be saved?
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Jesus replied,
What is impossible with man is possible with God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter said to him,
We have left all we had to follow you!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Truly I tell you,
no one who has left home or wife or brothers or sisters or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: will fail to receive many times as much in this age, and in the age to come eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Jesus Predicts His Death a Third Time

segments.text: Jesus took the Twelve aside and told them,
We are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written by the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He will be delivered over to the Gentiles. They will mock him, insult him and spit on him;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they will flog him and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The disciples did not understand any of this. Its meaning was hidden from them, and they did not know what he was talking about.

verses.title: A Blind Beggar Receives His Sight

segments.text: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
They told him,
Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Those who led the way rebuked him and told him to be quiet, but he shouted all the more,
Son of David, have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Jesus stopped and ordered the man to be brought to him. When he came near, Jesus asked him,
What do you want me to do for you?
Lord, I want to see.
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Receive your sight; your faith has healed you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they also praised God.
